merc.name: Mercenaries

subtypes.name: Reikland
subtypes.note: No special bonuses — the balanced standard.

subtypes.name: Middenheim
subtypes.note: Champions and the Captain start with Strength 4 instead of 3.

subtypes.name: Marienburg
subtypes.note: +100 gc (600 total) in the campaign. +1 when finding rare items.

merc.rules: Choice: Exactly 1 Captain. Max. 2 Champions, max. 2 Youngbloods, max. 7 Marksmen, max. 5 Swordsmen, any number of Warriors.

units.name: Mercenary Captain
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership.

units.name: Swordsman
units.sp: Expert Swordsmen: May re-roll missed to-hit rolls in close combat while armed with a sword.

possessed.name: Cult of the Possessed
possessed.rules: Choice: Exactly 1 Magister. Max. 2 Possessed, max. 2 Mutants, max. 5 Darksouls, max. 3 Beastmen, any number of Brethren.

units.name: Magister
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Wizard: Uses Chaos Rituals.

units.name: Possessed
units.sp: Fear. No weapons/armour. May take mutations (+ cost).

units.name: Mutant
units.sp: Must start with at least one mutation.

units.name: Darksoul
units.sp: Crazed: Automatically passes all Leadership tests.

wh.name: Witch Hunters
wh.rules: Choice: Exactly 1 Captain. Max. 1 Warrior Priest, max. 3 Witch Hunters, max. 5 Flagellants, max. 5 Warhounds, any number of Zealots.

units.name: Witch Hunter Captain
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Burn the Witch!: hates all spellcasters.

units.name: Warrior Priest
units.sp: Prayers of Sigmar. Burn the Witch!

units.name: Witch Hunter
units.sp: Burn the Witch!: hates all spellcasters.

units.name: Flagellant
units.sp: Fanatical: Automatically passes all Leadership tests; never the leader; never missile weapons.

units.name: Warhound
units.sp: Animal: Gains no experience. No weapons/armour.

sos.name: Sisters of Sigmar
sos.rules: Choice: Exactly 1 Matriarch. Max. 3 Sister Superiors, max. 1 Augur, max. 10 Novices, any number of Sisters.

units.name: Sigmarite Matriarch
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Prayers of Sigmar.

units.name: Augur
units.sp: Never wears armour. Blessed Sight: May re-roll dice; bonus when exploring.

undead.name: Undead
undead.rules: Choice: Exactly 1 Vampire. Max. 1 Necromancer, max. 3 Dregs, max. 5 Dire Wolves, any number of Zombies and Ghouls.

units.name: Vampire
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Fear. Immune to psychology & poison. No pain.

units.name: Necromancer
units.sp: Wizard: Uses Necromancy.

units.name: Zombie
units.sp: Fear. May not run. Immune to psychology/poison. No pain; no brain (gains no experience).

units.name: Ghoul
units.sp: Fear. Carries no equipment.

units.name: Dire Wolf
units.sp: Fear. Charge: 2 attacks on the turn it charges. May not run. Immune to psychology/poison. No pain. Gains no experience.

skaven.name: Skaven
skaven.rules: Choice: Exactly 1 Assassin Adept. Max. 1 Eshin Sorcerer, max. 2 Black Skaven, max. 2 Night Runners (Heroes), max. 1 Rat Ogre, any number of Verminkin and Giant Rats.

units.name: Assassin Adept
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Perfect Killer: An additional −1 to enemy armour saves (shooting & close combat).

units.name: Eshin Sorcerer
units.sp: Wizard: Magic of the Horned Rat.

units.name: Giant Rat
units.sp: No equipment. Gains no experience.

units.name: Rat Ogre
units.sp: Fear. Stupidity (unless a Skaven Hero is within 6"). Large Target. Gains no experience. (Rating 20)

woodelf.name: Wood Elves of Athel Loren
woodelf.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Hunt Master. Max. 2 Waywatchers, max. 1 Forest Mage, any number of Deepwood Scouts and Glade Guard.
Hate Dark Elves: All warriors (except Hired Swords) hate Dark Elves.
Excellent Sight: All Elves spot Hidden enemies from twice as far away (twice their Initiative in inches).
Unforgiving: Will not fight alongside the forces of Chaos; in multiplayer games may never ally with a Chaotic warband (Possessed, Skaven, Beastmen, Dark Elves, etc.).
Tolerant: may hire any Hired Sword that is not Chaotic or evil (no Skaven, Possessed, Beastmen, Dark Elves, Undead, etc.).

units.name: Hunt Master
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership.

units.name: Waywatcher
units.sp: Sharpshooter: May shoot while hidden (5+ to stay hidden). Camouflage: Requires an extra Initiative test to be spotted.

units.name: Forest Mage
units.sp: Wizard: Uses Woodland Incantations.

units.name: Deepwood Scout
units.sp: Crossfire: If several scouts shoot at the same target, +1 to the injury roll.

ostlander.name: Ostlanders
ostlander.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Elder. Max. 2 Blood Brothers, max. 1 Priest of Taal, max. 7 Hunters, max. 5 Ruffians, max. 1 Ogre, any number of Kin.
Self-sufficient: may not hire Hired Swords except Ogres.

units.name: Elder
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership.

units.name: Priest of Taal
units.sp: Prayers of Taal. May not wear heavy armour.

units.name: Ruffian
units.sp: Drunk: Automatically passes all Leadership tests; never the leader; no missile weapons.

units.name: Ogre
units.sp: Fear. Large Target. Slow-witted: Needs twice the experience for each advance (counts advances at half value). If promoted via The Lad's Got Talent, may only choose Combat and Strength skills.

carnival.name: Carnival of Chaos
carnival.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Carnival Master. Max. 2 Brutes, max. 2 Tainted Ones, max. 2 Plague Bearers, max. 1 Plague Cart, any number of Nurglings and Brethren.
Tainted: Counts as Possessed for exploration/injuries. No Hired Swords.
Blessings of Nurgle may be bought for Tainted Ones only at recruitment; the first is at listed cost, each further Blessing costs double.

units.name: Carnival Master
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Wizard: Uses Nurgle Rituals.

units.name: Brute
units.sp: Starts with the Strongman skill.

units.name: Tainted One
units.sp: Must start with at least one Blessing of Nurgle (+ cost).

units.name: Plague Bearer
units.sp: Daemon: Gains no experience. Fear. Immune to psychology/poison. Cloud of Flies, Stream of Corruption, Daemonic Aura 5+.

units.name: Nurgling
units.sp: Daemon (swarm, any number). Gains no experience. Immune to psychology/poison. Daemonic Aura 5+.

units.name: Plague Cart
units.sp: Cart (see cart rules). Increases the maximum number of warriors by +2. Daemonic.

dwarftreasure.name: Dwarf Treasure Hunters
dwarftreasure.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Noble. Max. 1 Engineer, max. 2 Slayers, max. 5 Thunderers, any number of Clansmen and Beardlings.
Hard to Kill · Hard Head · no movement penalty for armour · Hates Orcs & Goblins · +1 Wyrdstone in exploration.
Grudgebearers: Never any Elven Hired Swords or Dramatis Personae.
Gromril: Weapons cost only 3× at warband creation (later 4×, Rare 11).

units.name: Dwarf Noble
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership.

units.name: Engineer
units.sp: Master Weaponsmith: Increases the range of the warband's basic missile weapons (+3" pistols / +6" crossbows & handguns) while he takes part.

units.name: Troll Slayer
units.sp: Death Wish: Immune to psychology. No armour/missile weapons. Slayer skills.

averland.name: Averlanders
averland.rules: Choice: Exactly 1 Captain. Max. 1 Sergeant, 2 Mountain Hunters, 1 Youngblood; any number of Mountainguard and Marksmen, max. 3 Halfling Scouts.

units.name: Captain
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership.

units.name: Bergjaeger
units.sp: Set Traps: May lay a trap (S4 hit).

units.name: Halfling Scout
units.sp: Weak: May not choose Strength skills when advancing.

orcmob.name: Orc Mob
orcmob.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Boss. Max. 1 Shaman, 2 Big 'Uns; any number of Orc Boyz; Goblins (at most 2 per Orc in the warband), max. 5 Cave Squigs (never more than the Goblins), max. 1 Troll.
Animosity. Hired Swords only: Pit Fighter, Ogre Bodyguard, Warlock.

units.name: Orc Boss
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership.

units.name: Orc Shaman
units.sp: Wizard: Uses Waaagh! Magic. Never wears armour.

units.name: Orc Boyz
units.sp: Animosity.

units.name: Goblin
units.sp: Animosity. Not Orcs: Orcs expect little from non-Orcs — when testing whether the warband must take a Rout test, each Goblin or Cave Squig taken out of action counts as only HALF a model. Runts: Killed on a “The Lad’s Got Talent” result (too mouthy) — he may never become a Hero.

units.name: Cave Squig
units.sp: Animal (gains no experience). Move 2D6". Must stay within 6" of a Goblin, otherwise it goes wild. Not Orcs: When testing whether the warband must take a Rout test, each Cave Squig taken out of action counts as only HALF a model.

units.name: Troll
units.sp: Fear. Stupidity. Large Target. Regeneration (4+). Vomit Attack (S5). Always Hungry: 15 gc upkeep after each game. Gains no experience.

beastmen.name: Beastmen Raiders
beastmen.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Chief. Max. 1 Shaman, 2 Bestigors, 1 Centigor, 1 Minotaur, 5 Gors, 5 Chaos Hounds; any number of Ungors.
No Hired Swords (unless explicitly allowed).

units.name: Beastman Chief
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership.

units.name: Beastman Shaman
units.sp: Wizard: Uses Chaos Rituals. Never wears armour.

units.name: Centigor
units.sp: Drunk (test for stupidity/frenzy each turn). Woodland-dweller. Trample: +1 attack with no weapon bonuses.

units.name: Ungor
units.sp: Lowest of the low: Can never become a Hero.

units.name: Chaos Hound
units.sp: Animal: Gains no experience. No weapons/armour.

units.name: Minotaur
units.sp: Fear. Bloodgreed: If it takes all its enemies out of action in hand-to-hand, it becomes frenzied on a D6 roll of 4+. Animal: May gain experience but may never become a Hero. Large Target.

maraudersofchaos.name: Marauders of Chaos

subtypes.name: Norse
subtypes.note:
Norse.
Reavers: +1 when searching for rare items (post-game sequence).
Pantheon: The "Eye of the Gods" (Spawn/Mark) only triggers on 13+ instead of 12+.

subtypes.name: Kurgan
subtypes.note:
Kurgan.
Pedigree: Any number of Warhounds of Chaos (not just max. 5).
Bone Bows: Heroes and Chaos Marauder henchmen may carry bows (10 gc, common).
Difficult Customers: −1 when searching for rare items — except Great Axe and Barbed Whip.

subtypes.name: Hung
subtypes.note:
Hung.
Disloyalty: Max. 12 warriors instead of 15.
Affinity with Horses: Warhorses always cost only 40 gc; all Heroes (including promoted henchmen) automatically have "Ride Warhorse"; number of mounts unlimited (Border Town Burning).

maraudersofchaos.rules:
Choose a tribe (above): Norse, Kurgan or Hung — each with its own special rules.
Choice: Exactly 1 Chieftain. Max. 1 Seer, 1 Condemned, 2 Champions; any number of Chaos Marauders; max. 5 Warhounds (Kurgan: Any number); max. 1 Spawn.
Eye of the Gods: Roll 2D6 after each battle. Lost: +1 per own Hero taken out of action — on 12+ the leader becomes a Chaos Spawn. Won: +1 per enemy the leader took out of action — on 12+ a Mark of the Dark Gods (Serpent/Crow/Eagle/Arkhar/Undivided). With "Tattooed Body" already on 10+ (Norse 11+); pure Norse tribe 13+.
Hired Swords: Only Pit Fighters, Ogres, Norse Shamans, Imperial Assassins (Witches/Warlocks only without the Mark of Arkhar).

units.name: Marauder Chieftain
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership.

units.name: Seer
units.sp: Wizard: Uses Chaos magic via a Mark (Serpent/Crow/Eagle/Arkhar/Chaos Undivided), chosen when hired. Weapons only, no armour.

units.name: Condemned
units.sp: Fear. Inconsistency: WS, S, T and A are variable and re-rolled every turn. Fate: Fights without penalties; at 90 experience with values still variable he becomes a Chaos Spawn. (No equipment until values are fixed.)

units.name: Warhound of Chaos
units.sp: Animal: Gains no experience. No weapons/armour.

units.name: Spawn of Chaos
units.sp: Fear. Large Target. Special movement: 2D6" straight ahead (contact counts as a charge). Variable attacks (re-rolled each close-combat phase). Immune to all psychology. Gains no experience.

kislev.name: Kislevites
kislev.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Druzhina Captain. Max. 1 Bear Tamer, 1 Esaul, 2 Youths; any number of Warriors and Cossacks; max. 3 Streltsi; max. 1 Trained Bear (only with a Bear Tamer).
Hired Swords as Mercenaries. No alliance with Chaos. Hero special items (rare): Vodka, Bear-claw Necklace.
RAW: Esaul, Bear Tamer and Youths may only take weapons from the list (no armour).

units.name: Druzhina Captain
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Heirloom: May buy 1 item at half price when founding the warband (if lost, must be replaced at 150% — until then −1 to all tests and to-hit rolls).

units.name: Bear Tamer
units.sp: Bear Handler: Allows recruiting a Trained Bear.

units.name: Cossack
units.sp: Hates all Chaos warriors.

units.name: Streltsi
units.sp: Gun rest: +1 to hit with handgun + halberd if he did not move. The only ones allowed a handgun.

units.name: Trained Bear
units.sp: Large Target. Stupidity (auto-passed within 6" of the Bear Tamer; may only ever use the Tamer's Ld, never the leader's). Fear. Bear Hug. Fiercely Loyal: While the bear is not out of action, the Tamer treats Sold to the Pits/Robbed/Captured as Full Recovery. Animal: Gains no experience. Only usable if the Bear Tamer takes part.

amazons.name: Amazons (Mordheim)
amazons.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Priestess. Max. 2 Champions, 2 Totem Warriors; at least 1 Amazon Warrior, max. 3 Scouts.
Sacrifice: Captives are handled as by the Cult of the Possessed.
Not one of us: may never take Hired Swords or Dramatis Personae unless they are Amazons.

units.name: Priestess
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Wizard: Amazon Rituals (starts with one ritual chosen at random).

units.name: Totem Warrior
units.sp: Frenzy.

units.name: Scout
units.sp: Stealthy: Double deployment distance, starts hidden.

arabian.name: Arabian Tomb Raiders
arabian.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Sheikh. Max. 2 Champions, 1 Mystic; any number of Nomad Warriors, max. 2 Bedouins, max. 5 Slaves.
Hate Undead: All Araby Nomad Heroes hate every Undead model.

units.name: Sheikh
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests.

units.name: Nomadic Mystic
units.sp: Wizard: Uses Elementalism (the four elements — earth, wind, fire, water); starts with one random spell.

units.name: Bedouin
units.sp: Desert Trader: +1 modifier when the warband rolls to find Rare equipment (does not stack with other such bonuses).

units.name: Slave
units.sp: Life of Slavery: May gain experience, but on a Lad's got Talent roll the Sheikh executes the slave (remove from roster); the rest of the group re-rolls the advance.

blackorcs.name: Black Orcs
blackorcs.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Black Orc Boss. Max. 2 Black Orcs, 2 Young'uns; any number of Boyz, Shootaz (≤ Boyz), max. 4 Nuttaz, max. 1 Troll.
Animosity (Annual 2002: trigger on a D6 roll of 1).
Let the goons do the work: Black Orcs never ride mounts; only normal Orcs may ride a boar or similar.
Da Boss is Dead!: If the Boss is killed, a Black Orc always assumes leadership first (regardless of experience) and automatically gains the Oi Behave! skill.

units.name: Black Orc Boss
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Black Orc save 6+. Oi Behave!: if a Henchman fails Animosity within 6", the Boss may slap him — one automatic hit at a chosen Strength; if the Henchman stays standing, add that Strength to his Animosity Effect roll.

units.name: Black Orc
units.sp: Black Orc save 6+ (combines with armour).

units.name: Young'un
units.sp: Black Orc Blood: One Young'un in the warband may be upgraded for 10 gc — this allows him to take the 'Proven Warrior' special skill and become a full Black Orc.

units.name: Orc Boyz
units.sp: Animosity.

units.name: Orc Shootaz
units.sp: Animosity. (Number ≤ Boyz)

units.name: Orc Nuttaz
units.sp: Unstable: Does not suffer Animosity. Crazy: Automatically passes all Leadership-based tests; may never learn Academic skills if promoted. Savage: Must run/charge maximum distance towards the nearest visible enemy (friends do not block sight); fights with +1 extra Attack (not on profile); may never use armour or missile weapons; never Leader; may not use his Ld for Rout tests unless the last models on the board.

units.name: Troll
units.sp: Fear. Stupidity. Large Target. Regeneration (4+, not vs fire; no post-battle injury roll unless taken out by a flaming attack). Vomit Attack: May replace all attacks with one automatic S5 hit that ignores armour saves. Always Hungry: 20 gc upkeep after each game — or the Troll counts as 2 members for only 5 gc (max size effectively 11). Dumb Monster: Gains no experience.

bretonnian.name: Bretonnian Knights
bretonnian.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Questing Knight. Max. 2 Knights Errant, max. 3 Squires (never more Squires than Knights); max. 8 Men-at-Arms, any number of Bowmen.
Blessing of the Lady: Before each battle the leader takes a Ld test. If passed: Enemy models must roll 4+ on a D6 each time they wish to fire a black powder weapon, and any enemy shooting at a Questing Knight or Knight Errant must first roll 4+ to overcome the curse.

units.name: Questing Knight
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Knight's Virtue: Never panics and breaks from combat; no Leadership test for being All Alone.

units.name: Knight Errant
units.sp: Knight's Virtue: A chivalrous warrior superior to ordinary men — never panics or breaks from combat, and never has to take an All Alone test. May not ride a warhorse unless the Questing Knight also rides one.

units.name: Squire
units.sp: May not ride a horse unless the Questing Knight and all Knights Errant ride warhorses.

darkelves.name: Dark Elves
darkelves.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 High Born. Max. 1 Beastmaster, 2 Fellblades, 1 Sorceress; any number of Corsairs, max. 5 Shades, max. 2 Cold One Beasthounds (only with a Beastmaster).
Kindred Hatred: Hate all High Elf warriors (including High Elf Hired Swords).
Excellent Sight: All Elves spot Hidden enemies at twice the normal distance (twice their Initiative in inches).
No black powder: Dark Elves find such weapons too crude and never use them.

units.name: High Born
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests.

units.name: Beastmaster
units.sp: Allows Cold One Beasthounds.

units.name: Fellblade
units.sp: Close-combat specialist.

units.name: Sorceress
units.sp: Wizard: Uses Dark Elf Magic. Armour allowed but she may not cast spells in any turn she is wearing armour.

units.name: Shade
units.sp: Natural Stealth.

units.name: Cold One Beasthound
units.sp: Fear. Scaly skin 6+. Animal (gains no experience). Only with a Beastmaster.

dwarfrangers.name: Dwarf Rangers
dwarfrangers.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Runesmith. Max. 1 Apprentice, 2 Troll Slayers; max. 5 Longbeards, any number of Clansmen, max. 5 Crossbowmen, any number of Beardlings.
Dwarf rules: Hard to Kill (injury 1-2 knocked down, 3-5 stunned, only 6 = out of action) · Hard Head · no movement penalty for armour · Hates Orcs & Goblins · Grudgebearers (never Elven Hired Swords/DPs) · Incomparable Miners (+1 wyrdstone).
Don’t Trust ‘Em: Members of a Ranger warband are never ‘friendly models’ to other Dwarf warbands (and vice versa) in multi-player games.

units.name: Runesmith
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Inscribe Runes: Knows 1 random minor rune; before each game roll 2D6 ≥ difficulty to inscribe it on one item (fumble on 2 destroys the item); may learn new runes instead of skills. Rune bonuses do not stack with other magic items.

units.name: Apprentice Runesmith
units.sp: Extra Set of Hands: While in the warband, the Runesmith may inscribe runes on two items before each game instead of one.

units.name: Troll Slayer
units.sp: Deathwish: Completely immune to all psychology and never tests for fighting alone.

units.name: Longbeard
units.sp: Stubborn: May reroll any failed Leadership test (never reroll a reroll).

forestgoblins.name: Forest Goblins
forestgoblins.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Chieftain. Max. 4 Braves, 1 Shaman; any number of Forest Goblins, max. 5 Red Toof Boyz, max. 5 Sluggas, max. 1 Gigantic Spider.
Animosity. Natives (no penalty in woods).

units.name: Chieftain
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. May Ride: With the optional mount rules the Chieftain may ride a Giant Spider or the warband's Gigantic Spider — on the Gigantic Spider he gains +1 armour save, moves at its rate of 6, and shooting hits the Chieftain on 1-2, the spider on 3-6.

units.name: Brave
units.sp: Animosity. May remove his Animosity instead of taking a skill when he earns one.

units.name: Shaman
units.sp: Wizard: Forest Goblin Magic (starts with 1).

units.name: Forest Goblin
units.sp: Animosity.

units.name: Red Toof Boyz
units.sp: Frenzy: Subject to frenzy (tribal jewellery — cannot be removed). Animosity: Subject to Animosity, but immune to it while starting the turn within charge range of an enemy.

units.name: Slugga
units.sp: Animosity. Sluggas: May throw thrown weapons up to three times per shooting phase (may not be combined with Quick Shot if promoted).

units.name: Gigantic Spider
units.sp: Fear. Large Target: May always be shot at even if not the closest target. Poisonous: When it wounds, roll injuries with 1 = knocked down, 2-4 = stunned, 5-6 = out of action. Nonsentient: Subject to Stupidity; never gains experience (animal); may climb as normal. Native: Moves through woods without penalty. Uses no weapons or armour. May be ridden by the Chieftain.

gunnery.name: Gunnery School of Nuln
gunnery.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Senior Gunnery Officer. Max. 1 Instructor, 1 Senior Student, 2 Underclassmen; any number of Sons of the Guns, max. 7 Marksmen, max. 5 Pistoliers.
Impeccable Care: Black powder weapons ALWAYS at the reduced list prices (also later purchases); +2 on rare rolls to find any black powder weapon.
Properly Used: With the optional Misfire rules, on a roll of 1 roll again — on 3+ the misfire is ignored (shot misses, weapon does not explode).
Proud To A Fault: Members NEVER use non-black-powder missile weapons (does not apply to Hired Swords/Dramatis Personae).

units.name: Senior Gunnery Officer
units.sp: Leader (12"): friendly models within 12" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Hunter: As the Shooting skill — may fire a black powder weapon every turn instead of every other turn.

units.name: Instructor
units.sp: Expert Weaponsmith: While in the warband, all pistol weapons get +3" range and all other black powder weapons +6". Chooses equipment from BOTH the School and the Marksman list.

units.name: Marksman
units.sp: Quick Reload: Has the Hunter skill (fire black powder weapons every turn); no additional effect from taking Hunter again if promoted.

units.name: Pistolier
units.sp: Crack Shot: When using pistols in close combat, may re-roll any missed to-hit rolls in the first round of combat.

hochland.name: Hochland Bandits
hochland.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Bandit Prince. Max. 2 Footpads, 1 Duelist, 1 Huckster; any number of Thugs, max. 4 Looters, max. 2 Blackhearts, max. 2 Poachers, any number of Gutterscum.
Hired Swords: may hire any Hired Swords allowed to a Human Mercenary warband.
Powder's Expensive!: Black powder weapons only at the marked-up list prices, and only for the Duelist — henchmen may never buy them.
Foragers: When determining income, always use the next lower warband-size column (1-3 members still use the first column).
Know Who To Sell To: When selling equipment, the warband gets half of any random price element in addition to half the basic cost.

units.name: Bandit Prince
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests.

units.name: Footpad
units.sp: Concealment: An enemy charging a Footpad he cannot see (not Hidden) takes his Initiative test at −1, and a roll of 6 always fails; when detecting a HIDING Footpad, enemies halve their Initiative for the range check.

units.name: Duelist
units.sp: Swordmaster: Parries succeed on a roll EQUAL to or over the enemy's to-hit roll (needs a parry weapon). Weapon Knowledge: The Duelist list is only his starting gear — he may use ANY close combat or missile weapon the warband gets hold of.

units.name: Huckster
units.sp: Convincing Manner: Enemies charging a warband member within 12" of the Huckster must first pass a Ld test or stand (failed charge); models with Stupidity and animals are immune. Slick Operator: Between games roll a D6 — on 2-6 the warband gains 2D6 gc, on 1 the Huckster misses the next game (may not be combined with the Banditry skill).

units.name: Looter
units.sp: Looting the Dead: After the battle, for each warrior (from ANY warband, own included) removed from his roster, roll a D6 per Looter — on 4+ the Bandit player keeps all of that warrior's equipment (each warrior lootable only once).

units.name: Blackheart
units.sp: Hardened (immune to fear/all alone).

units.name: Poacher
units.sp: Trailblazers: The warband may REROLL one D6 in the Exploration phase for each Poacher.

units.name: Gutterscum
units.sp: Utterly Inept (gains no experience).

hornedhunters.name: Horned Hunters
hornedhunters.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Horned Hunter. Max. 1 Priest of Taal, 3 Initiates; max. 5 Drunken Gang, any number of Zealots, max. 5 Warhounds.
Woodcraft: Followers of Taal move through any difficult terrain without suffering movement penalties.

units.name: Horned Hunter
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Hang the Bandit!: hates all Bandits and Outlaws (follows the Hatred rules against them).

units.name: Priest of Taal
units.sp: Prayers: Uses the Prayers of Taal (works like priestly prayers). Strictures: May never wear heavy armour (light armour, helmet and shield are allowed — mordheimer clarification).

units.name: Initiate
units.sp: Infiltrator: Always set up after the opposing warband, anywhere out of sight and more than 12" from any enemy (if both sides infiltrate, roll off — lowest sets up first). Hang the Bandit!: hates all Bandits and Outlaws. Strictures: May never wear armour.

units.name: Drunken Gang
units.sp: Drunk: Automatically passes Leadership tests.

units.name: Zealot
units.sp: No armour.

units.name: Warhound
units.sp: Animal (gains no experience).

outriders.name: Imperial Outriders
outriders.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Knight. Max. 2 Outriders, 2 Scouts; max. 5 Chasseurs, 5 Hussars, 2 Grooms.
Mounted warband: Every warrior comes with a Riding Horse (included in the cost); any may upgrade it to a Warhorse for +40 gc. All use the Blazing Saddles cavalry rules (Ride / Combat Riding / Horse Handling).
Mounted combat: A mounted warrior may not fight with two close combat weapons (he needs one hand for the reins) and may never use two-handed weapons; a shield or buckler is fine, and a pistol may be fired in the first round of close combat in place of a weapon.
Dense terrain: Imperial Outriders ignore the normal two-mount warband limit in areas of dense terrain.
Hired Swords: Only mounted Hired Swords may join — the Freelance Knight (Rulebook) and the Roadwarden (Empire in Flames); the Highwayman keeps his distance and may never be hired.

units.name: Knight
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Ride. Riding horse; warhorse upgrade +40.

units.name: Outrider
units.sp: Ride. Riding horse; warhorse +40.

units.name: Scout
units.sp: Ride. Riding horse.

units.name: Chasseur
units.sp: Ride. Riding horse.

units.name: Hussar
units.sp: Ride, Combat Riding. Riding horse; warhorse +40.

units.name: Groom
units.sp: Ride, Horse Handling. Riding horse.

lizardmen.name: Lizardmen
lizardmen.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Skink Priest. Max. 1 Saurus Totem Warrior, 2 Skink Great Crests; any number of Skink Braves, max. 4 Saurus Braves (never more than Skink Braves), max. 1 Kroxigor.
Cold Blooded: Roll 3D6 and pick the lowest 2 for psychology/Rout tests; the warband may never use the Leadership of Saurus or Kroxigor for Rout tests. Scaly Skin: Saurus 5+, Skinks 6+ (cannot be modified below 6; light armour/shield add +1). Armour is rare: Light armour always costs 50 gc. Skinks: Aquatic & Jungle born. Town Cryer #11 version (Lustria).

units.name: Skink Priest
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Wizard: Uses Lizardman Magic (works like priestly prayers, castable in armour).

units.name: Saurus Totem Warrior
units.sp: Scaly Skin 5+. Bite attack: +1 extra attack at own Strength (no weapon penalty, always strikes last).

units.name: Saurus Brave
units.sp: Scaly Skin 5+. Bite attack: +1 extra attack at own Strength (always strikes last). (Number ≤ Skink Braves)

units.name: Kroxigor
units.sp: Halberd only. Scaly 4+, Aquatic, Fear, Large Target, Animal (gains no experience), counts as 2 models.

mootlanders.name: Mootlanders
mootlanders.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Moot Elder. Max. 1 Master Chef, 3 Halfling Thieves; max. 5 Scouts, any number of Halfling Warriors.
Puny: When rolling for a Halfling's injury, treat a roll of 2 as 'stunned'. Citadel Journal 36.

units.name: Moot Elder
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. May also buy a pistol (15 gc, family heirloom).

units.name: Master Chef
units.sp: Inspired Cooking: Halflings within 6" may re-roll failed to-hit rolls in close combat (once per turn).

units.name: Halfling Thief
units.sp: Sneaky: −1 to hit when shot at (cumulative with other modifiers).

units.name: Halfling Scout
units.sp: Keen Eyesight: Spots hidden enemies at twice his Initiative in inches.

norse.name: Norse Explorers
norse.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Jarl. Max. 2 Berserkers, 1 Wulfen, 2 Bondsmen; any number of Marauders, max. 5 Hunters, max. 5 Wolves (only with a Wulfen).
Seafaring: All Norse warriors get +2 Strength when trying to row a boat.

units.name: Jarl
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests.

units.name: Berserker
units.sp: Frenzy: Subject to frenzy (see Psychology).

units.name: Wulfen
units.sp: Fear, Bestial (immune to psychology), can never become the leader. No weapons/armour.

units.name: Wolf
units.sp: Animal (gains no experience). Only usable with a Wulfen (Pack Leader).

ostermark.name: Ostermarkers
ostermark.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Captain. Max. 2 Champions, 2 Youngbloods; any number of Warriors, max. 7 Marksmen, max. 5 Swordsmen.
Hardy: Heroes & henchmen may re-roll one failed Ld test for 'All Alone' and Rout (must accept the second roll). The Captain may take a Wardog.
Skills: Ostermark was published without a skill table — by official clarification the player picks one of the three Mercenary skill sets (Reikland / Middenheim / Marienburg). Offered here: The union of those sets — Captain all five, Champion/Youngblood Combat/Shooting/Strength/Speed (Academic is in none of the three sets). Strictly, pick one set and stick to it.

units.name: Captain
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Hardy.

units.name: Champion
units.sp: Hardy.

units.name: Youngblood
units.sp: Hardy.

units.name: Warrior
units.sp: Hardy.

units.name: Marksman
units.sp: Hardy.

units.name: Swordsman
units.sp: Expert Swordsman. Hardy.

units.name: Wardog (Captain)
units.sp: Animal (gains no experience). Only 1, with the Captain.

outlaws.name: Outlaws of Stirwood Forest
outlaws.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Bandit Leader. Max. 2 Champions, 1 Cleric (instead of a Champion/Petty Thief), 2 Petty Thieves; max. 7 Marksmen, any number of Outlaws.
Bow duty: Every warrior carries a bow (no crossbow), only one missile weapon (exception: the Cleric may but need not carry a bow).
Hired Swords: Bounty Hunter, Wolf-Priest of Ulric, Norse Shaman and Dark Elf Assassin are not available.

units.name: Bandit Leader
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests.

units.name: Cleric
units.sp: Disciple of Sigmar: Starts a campaign knowing one of the Prayers of Sigmar (toggle him as a caster to pick it). As with a Witch-Hunter's Warrior-Priest he may learn neither Sorcery nor Arcane Lore. May wear armour, since Prayers are not Spells. Exempt from Bow Duty: He may carry a bow but need not.

pirates.name: Pirates
pirates.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Ship's Captain. Max. 2 Mates, 2 Cabin Boys; any number of Crew, max. 7 Gunners, max. 5 Boatswains, max. 5 Swabbies (≤ Crew).
Swivel Gun: Max. 1 per warband.
Swabbies: Recruited via special rules (Kidnapped!, Stragglers/Prisoners — starting cost here 0 gc); never more Swabbies than Crew.
Hired Swords: As Mercenaries, but +20 gc upkeep if Dwarfs and Elves serve together.
One-off games: Start with two free Swabbies.
Succession: A Mate takes over if the Captain dies.

units.name: Ship's Captain
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests.

units.name: Gunner
units.sp: Swivel Gun max. 1/warband; the bearer operates alone.

units.name: Boatswain
units.sp: Starts with Rope & Hook; Expert Riggers.

units.name: Swabbie
units.sp: Special recruitment; gains no experience; Rabble.

pitfighters.name: Pit Fighters
pitfighters.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Pit King. Max. 1 Dwarf Troll Slayer, 2 Veterans, 1 Ogre; max. 7 Pursuers, any number of Pit Fighters.
Pit Fighter skill: All warriors have it (+1 WS and +1 A when fighting in ruins, buildings or the pit).
Bulging Biceps: A Pit Fighter with this special skill ignores the whole 'heavy' weapon penalty, and its Strength bonus then applies in EVERY close-combat round (e.g. a morning star gives +1 Strength every turn, not just the first).
Fighting styles: Weapons and armour are not chosen freely — each Man Pit Fighter picks one fixed fighting-style kit (Troll Slayer and Ogre have their own limited list).
Free the Slaves: Pit Fighters hate all slavers and never sell captured opponents to them.
In the Pit: A captured enemy may instead be made to fight a warband member in the pits (win: that fighter +2 Exp and the warband gets the captive's gear +50 gc; lose: roll for the fighter's injury).
Hired Swords: Pit Fighters may hire any Hired Sword except the Elf Ranger.

units.name: Pit King
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Pit Fighter.

units.name: Dwarf Troll Slayer
units.sp: Pit Fighter skill. Death Wish (immune to all psychology). Hard to Kill (only taken out of action on a 6). Hard Head (ignores mace/club concussion). Hates Orcs & Goblins. Grudgebearers: Leaves the warband if any Elf is hired. May never use missile weapons or armour.

units.name: Pit Veteran
units.sp: Pit Fighter.

units.name: Ogre Pit Fighter
units.sp: Pit Fighter skill. Fear. Large Target. Slow-witted: Needs twice the experience for each advance. If promoted via The Lad's Got Talent, may only choose Combat, Strength and Pit Fighter Special Skills.

units.name: Pit Fighter
units.sp: Pit Fighter.

units.name: Pursuer
units.sp: Evade; Pit Fighter.

skavenpest.name: Skaven of Clan Pestilens
skavenpest.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Plague Priest. Max. 1 Pestilens Sorcerer, 2 Plague Monks, 2 Monk Initiates; any number of Plague Novices, any number of Giant Rats, max. 1 Rat Ogre.
Skaven base rules.

units.name: Plague Priest
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests.

units.name: Pestilens Sorcerer
units.sp: Wizard (Magic of the Horned Rat).

units.name: Giant Rat
units.sp: Animal (gains no experience).

units.name: Rat Ogre
units.sp: Fear, Stupidity (unless a Skaven Hero is within 6"), Large Target, gains no experience.

tileans.name: Tileans

subtypes.name: Miragliano
subtypes.note:
Crossbow Experts: Miraglean Heroes get +1 to hit when using crossbows; Marksmen get +1 to hit with any missile weapon.
Hatred (Skaven): All Miragleans hate Skaven (the red pox of 1812) — Hired Swords do not benefit.
Hired Swords: A Miraglean warband may not hire Skaven Hired Swords.

subtypes.name: Remas
subtypes.note:
Kindred Hatred (Dark Elves): A Remasen warband fights to the death against Dark Elves — may re-roll one failed Rout test when fighting them (must keep the second roll).
Superior Leadership: Captain, Champions and Youngbloods have +1 Leadership (applied automatically to their profiles).
Hired Swords: A Remasen warband may not hire Dark Elf Hired Swords.

subtypes.name: Trantio
subtypes.note: Best Equipped: The wealthiest, most experienced human warband in Lustria — starts with 600 gc (a one-off game gives +100 gc; a campaign gives +20%, both 600 from the base 500).

tileans.rules:
Choose a city (above): Miragliano, Remas or Trantio — each with its own special rules.
Choice: Exactly 1 Captain. Max. 2 Champions, 2 Youngbloods; any number of Warriors, max. 5 Duellists, max. 7 Marksmen.

units.name: Captain
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests.

units.name: Duellist
units.sp: Cloak & Dagger (counts as a buckler in close combat).

tombguardians.name: Tomb Guardians (Khemri)
tombguardians.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Tomb Lord. Max. 1 Liche Priest, 2 Acolytes; any number of Skeleton Warriors, max. 2 Tomb Guardians, max. 3 Tomb Scorpions.
Undead: Fear, immune to psychology, No Pain (stunned counts as knocked down), may not run, immune to poison.
Home Ground: +1 die in the Exploration phase.
No Brain: Skeletons never gain experience (Tomb Lord, Liche Priest, Acolytes and Tomb Guardians do).
Do not Drink: Undead need no food or water; living animals with the warband follow the water rules as normal.

units.name: Tomb Lord
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Undead. Flammable (fire = double wounds). Does not learn new skills — rather 'remembers' those he knew in life.

units.name: Liche Priest
units.sp: Wizard: Uses Mortuary Cult Scrolls. Undead: Follows all Undead rules. Weapons only, no armour (interferes with spellcasting).

units.name: Acolyte
units.sp: Undead: Follows all Undead rules.

units.name: Skeleton Warrior
units.sp: Undead, gains no experience.

units.name: Tomb Guardian
units.sp: Undead: Follows all Undead rules. Gains experience: The original source explicitly notes Tomb Guards DO gain experience (Mordheimer: Use the Tomb Lord maximum characteristics).

units.name: Tomb Scorpion
units.sp: Living, Animal (gains no experience), poison sting (as Black Lotus).

amazonslustria.name: Amazons (Lustria)
amazonslustria.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Serpent Priestess. Max. 2 Eagle Warriors, 2 Piranha Warriors; any number of Amazon Warriors (at least 1), max. 3 Jaguar Warriors.
Isolationists: may re-roll missed attacks in the FIRST round of close combat against Lizardmen and Norse.
Sacrifice: Captives are handled as by the Cult of the Possessed; sacrificing a captured Lizardman additionally grants a free Enchanted Skins (Skins & Charms).
Norse Enmity: Against a Norse warband the Amazons fight to the death — reroll the first failed Rout test and may never voluntarily rout unless the leader is Out of Action.
Not one of us: may never take Hired Swords or Dramatis Personae unless they are Amazons.

units.name: Serpent Priestess
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests. Wizard (Amazon Rituals, 1 random).

units.name: Piranha Warrior
units.sp: Conch Shell Horn: At the start of the game the warband may re-roll the roll for who deploys first / goes first (multiple horns give no extra re-roll).

units.name: Jaguar Warrior
units.sp: One with the Jungle: Moves through jungle/wood terrain without any movement penalty.

shadowwarriors.name: Shadow Warriors
shadowwarriors.rules:
Choice: Exactly 1 Shadow Master. Max. 3 Shadow Walkers, 1 Shadow Weaver; any number of Shadow Warriors, any number of Shadow Novices.
Hate Dark Elves: All warriors (except Hired Swords) hate Dark Elves.
Excellent Sight: Elves spot Hidden enemies from twice as far away (twice their Initiative in inches).
Distaste for Poison: warriors may not use poisons or drugs of any type.
Unforgiving: In multiplayer games may never ally with a Chaotic warband (Possessed, Skaven, Beastmen, Dark Elves, etc.).
Tolerant: may hire any Hired Sword that is not Chaotic or evil (no Skaven, Possessed, Beastmen, Dark Elves, Undead, etc.) and no poison-users (no Assassins).

units.name: Shadow Master
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests.

units.name: Shadow Weaver
units.sp: Wizard: Uses Shadow Magic. May be equipped with weapons AND armour, but may not cast spells while wearing armour.

sonsofhashut.name: The Sons of Hashut
sonsofhashut.rules: Chaos Dwarf slavers of Hashut. Hard Head: Chaos Dwarfs & Bull Centaurs ignore the special rules of maces, clubs and hammers. Armour: Chaos Dwarfs & Bull Centaurs never suffer movement penalties for wearing armour. Hard to Kill: Chaos Dwarfs & Bull Centaurs are taken out of action only on a 6 (Injury 1-2 = knocked down, 3-5 = stunned, 6 = OOA). Slavers: Captured fighters are never freed — sacrifice them (Apprentice Sorcerer +1 Experience each) or put them to work (+1 wyrdstone each, then D6: 2-6 the captive dies and you keep its equipment; 1 it escapes). Uncommon: Chaos Dwarf recruits cost 1.5× the usual Experience for veterancy (rounding up); Hobgoblins are normal. Indentured Servants: The warband must include at least four Hobgoblins; while below four you may recruit only Hobgoblins. Hired Swords: Ogre Bodyguard, Pit Fighter, Warlock, Imperial Assassin, Hobgoblin Scout, plus any "all may hire" or those open to Orc & Chaos warbands — never Elves of any sort.

units.name: Apprentice Sorcerer
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership. Wizard: Uses Chaos Dwarf Magic — he cannot cast spells while wearing armour. Hard Head. Hard to Kill.

units.name: Bull Centaur
units.sp: Large Target. Hard Head. Hard to Kill. No movement penalty for wearing armour.

units.name: Chaos Dwarf Champion
units.sp: Hard Head. Hard to Kill.

units.name: Chaos Dwarf Warrior
units.sp: Hard Head. Hard to Kill.

units.name: Chaos Dwarf Blunderbusser
units.sp: Hard Head. Hard to Kill. May carry a Chaos Dwarf Blunderbuss.

units.name: Hobgoblin
units.sp: Skinny: On a Serious Injury roll of 1-3 the Hobgoblin leaves the warband. Nobody cares: Counts as half a model when making Rout tests. Cannot be promoted to Hero.

blackdwarfs.name: Black Dwarfs
blackdwarfs.rules: Chaos Dwarf slavers from the Border Town Burning supplement. The following apply to all warriors except Informers: Hard to Kill: Taken out of action only on a 6 (Injury 1-2 = knocked down, 3-5 = stunned, 6 = OOA). Hard Head: Ignore the special rules of clubs, maces, etc. Armour: Never suffer movement penalties for wearing armour. Hired Swords: Ogre Bodyguard, Pit Fighter, Warlock, Imperial Assassin, Hobgoblin Scout, plus any "all may hire" or those open to Orc & Chaos warbands — never Elves of any sort. The Engine of Chaos and special Chaos Dwarf wargear (Man-catcher, Mechanical Suit, Chaos Armour, Obsidian Weapon) are bought from the Trading Post / rare-item picker.

units.name: Sorcerer
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership. Wizard: Uses Rituals of Hashut. Priest: Starts with two rituals — the Sacrificial Ritual plus one more randomly generated from the Rituals of Hashut.

units.name: Bull Centaur
units.sp: Large Target. May never use missile weapons. Hard Head. Hard to Kill. No movement penalty for wearing armour.

units.name: Gaoler
units.sp: Nasty Reputation: The dreadful Gaolers cause fear in Humans. Hard Head. Hard to Kill.

units.name: Informer
units.sp: Drudgery: Informers may never become Heroes (re-roll "The Lad's Got Talent"). They are not subject to the Chaos Dwarf special rules (Hard Head, Hard to Kill, Armour).

units.name: Chaos Dwarf
units.sp: Hard Head. Hard to Kill.

battlemonks.name: Battle Monks of Cathay
battlemonks.rules: Cathayan warrior-monks of the Border Town Burning supplement. Strictures: Dragon Monks and Warrior Monks never wear any kind of armour. Distaste for Poison: Dragon Monks and Warrior Monks may never use any poison or venom. Outsiders: The warband may never hire any Hired Sword or Dramatis Personae unless that entry specifically allows it. Cathayans are Humans and use the Human racial maximum.

units.name: Emissary
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership. Ride Horse: Trained to ride Horses. Decree: If the Emissary dies, a new one must be hired before any other warrior or equipment is bought; he then reclaims leadership. From the special skills he may only ever pick Warmonger.

units.name: Officer
units.sp: A decorated soldier and custodian of Imperial law. (Equipped with weapons from the Soldier list — no armour.)

units.name: Dragon Monk
units.sp: Never wears armour. Unarmed: No penalties whatsoever and +1 Attack when fighting unarmed. Art of Silent Death: When fighting unarmed, scores a critical hit on a to-wound roll of 5-6 instead of 6 (with a quarter staff, only the unarmed attacks crit on 5+).

units.name: Soldier
units.sp: Trained border guards and local militia.

units.name: Warrior Monk
units.sp: Never wears armour. Unarmed: No penalties whatsoever and +1 Attack when fighting unarmed.

units.name: Raging Peasant
units.sp: Fights unarmed without penalty. Simple folk: Never gains Experience. Mob: +1 Leadership for each other friendly Peasant within 3"; does not benefit from the Leader rule. Ignored: Peasants out of action are not counted for Rout tests. Downtrodden: When wounded, do not roll for injury — the model is immediately taken out of action.

bretchapel.name: Bretonnian Chapel Guard
bretchapel.rules: Fan-made Bretonnian warband (based on Tom Merrigan's Bretonnians). Chivalry: Knights may never use any missile weapon (except Holy Water), nor any drug or poison, nor learn spells (prayers are allowed). Lord's Boon: Each Knight may, on purchase, start with ONE of a Warhorse, Light Armour OR Heavy Armour at half cost — it can never be traded, given away or sold, and is removed from the warband if the Knight dies. Virtue of Purity: Knights may never voluntarily break from combat unless knocked down, and are immune to All Alone and similar mundane effects (magical fear effects still work). Bretonnians use the Human racial maximum.

units.name: Questing Knight
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership. Knight: Subject to Chivalry, Lord's Boon and Virtue of Purity. Ride: Starts with the Ride Warhorse skill. Vow of Poverty: May not take a Lance. Uses no missile weapons and learns no spells.

units.name: Damsel
units.sp: Prayercaster: Uses the Prayers of the Lady (Lady's Prayers) and starts with one prayer; she may learn more as a wizard does. Prayers are not spells.

units.name: Knight Errant
units.sp: Knight: Subject to Chivalry, Lord's Boon and Virtue of Purity. Vain: May not wear a helmet. Impetuous: If not in combat and it did not charge this turn, it must charge a viable standing enemy in range (auto-passing psychology for that charge; you may take an Ld test to act normally instead). Uses no missile weapons.

units.name: Squire
units.sp: Knighthood: On "The Lad's Got Talent" you may keep him a Squire (choose two of Combat/Academic/Strength/Speed) or promote him to a Knight Errant (gains Knight/Vain/Impetuous, switches to the Knight list, no missile weapons — this may exceed the 3-Knight-Errant limit). A Squire may ride a horse only if the Questing Knight and every Knight Errant ride warhorses.

units.name: Battle Pilgrim
units.sp: Low Caste: Advances normally but re-rolls "The Lad's Got Talent" and may never become a Hero. Hatred: Hates all enemies. Stubborn: Re-roll all failed Leadership tests once, accepting the second result. Holy Relics: May carry a Holy Relic (henchman exception); a Pilgrim with one gains Frenzy (and is then immune to Hatred) and, if needed, splits off into his own henchman group.

units.name: Bowman
units.sp: Low Caste: Advances normally but re-rolls "The Lad's Got Talent" and may never become a Hero.

cppleasures.name: Court of the Profane Pleasures
cppleasures.rules:
Draft / work-in-progress: Slaaneshi pleasure-cult by Tuomas Pirinen (unofficial); no official skill table was ever published — the skill lists below are placeholders.
Choice: At least one Hero must act as Leader (pick any); you may hire only one of each Hero type. Corruption of the Mind and Flesh: When any model dies you may pay 1 wyrdstone to graft one of its characteristics onto a Slaaneshi Beastman, Cultist or Hound (one Hero then cannot search the rare market that post-battle). Mutations: Any Hero may buy ONE of Extra Arm, Tentacle or Great Claw at recruitment (see Cult of the Possessed mutations). Humans; Beastmen use the Beastman maximum.

units.name: Slaaneshi Whipmaster
units.sp: Agony and Ecstasy: Armed with a Hedonist Whip he may spend one attack to scourge a friendly model within 3" (auto-hit, +1 to wound, roll injuries normally); in reward he doubles his Toughness until he fails an Ld test at the start of one of his turns. Pain and Pleasure: Whenever he suffers an unsaved wound he is subject to Frenzy until the end of his next turn (not negated by being knocked down or stunned).

units.name: Slaaneshi Danseuse
units.sp: Never wears armour. Strange Allure: An enemy wishing to attack her in melee or with a missile must first pass a Leadership test; on a failure it must re-roll any successful hits.

units.name: Slaaneshi Flesh Merchant
units.sp: Pound of Flesh: Before the battle a Wretch may be carried as a "meat shield" — when the Merchant would suffer a wound, the Wretch is taken out of action instead (it can still attack in melee, has no base and cannot be targeted; lost if the Merchant dies). Cruel Fate: Any captives gained may be turned into Wretches for free.

units.name: Slaaneshi Priest of Obscene
units.sp: Wizard: Uses the Chaos Rituals spell list. Flesh Reserve: Before the battle a Wretch may be carried as a "sacrificial pawn" — when the Priest fails to cast a spell he may sacrifice the Wretch to re-roll the casting (it can still attack in melee, has no base and cannot be targeted; lost if the Priest dies).

units.name: Slaaneshi Devout
units.sp: Fluctuating Form: At the start of the battle and each Recovery phase (if not in close combat) choose Male (+1 WS, S, T, A) or Female (+1 BS, +2 I, +2 Ld, +2 M). Immune to Psychology and All Alone tests.

units.name: Slaaneshi Chaos Hound
units.sp: Animal: Cannot use equipment, weapons or armour, and never gains Experience.

units.name: Slaaneshi Beastman
units.sp: A Beastman of Slaanesh — more agile and humanlike than common Gors.

units.name: Cultist
units.sp: A depraved member of the pleasure court.

units.name: Slaaneshi Wretch
units.sp: Slaves to Darkness: Immune to all psychology and All Alone tests while within 6" of a friendly Hero. Wretches can never become Heroes — if "The Lad's Got Talent" is rolled and then rolled again, the model instead takes a Serious Injury (rolled at once, as a Hero).

cavalcade.name: The Cursed Cavalcade
cavalcade.rules: Masked, cursed nobles of Mordheim (Tuomas Pirinen). Foolish Nobleness: Counts as an Evil warband (like the Cult of the Possessed for all game effects, e.g. result 333). May hire no Hired Swords or Dramatis Personae except the Crow Master (and any that specifically allow it). Capture!: If a Hero takes an enemy Human Henchman Out of Action with a Misericordia and you have fewer than 5 Captured Thralls, on a 5+ the model is captured; enemy Heroes only via "61 - Captured!"; max 2 captures per game. The Throne of Worms: For each captive, roll D6 instead of Serious Injuries — 1-2 destroyed, 3-5 you gain a Captured Thrall, 6 sacrificed (a random Hero +1 XP). Cursed Masks: Each Hero may take one mask free at warband creation (one of each kind only); afterwards masks may be bought (see the rare-item picker). Humans (animals do not advance).

units.name: Aristocrat
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership. Expert Horseman: Has the Ride (Nightmare) skill.

units.name: Companion
units.sp: Expert Swordsman: Every Companion has the Expert Swordsman skill.

units.name: Twisted Scholar
units.sp: Magical Adept: When hired, you may pay +10 gc to make him a Wizard (uses Lesser Magic, starts with one random spell). Story Teller: Alternatively pay +10 gc to make him a Chronicler (re-roll one extra Exploration die and choose which two dice to keep). Toggle "caster" below if upgraded to a Wizard.

units.name: Cursed Piper
units.sp: Plays an instrument one-handed: may not use an off-hand weapon, double-handed weapon, brace of pistols, bows or crossbows. Danse Macabre: In the shooting phase, target one enemy within 6" who must pass an Ld test or be controlled (moved anywhere, even out of combat or off heights); it tests Ld each recovery phase to break free and meanwhile cannot shoot, charge, cast or attack. Only one victim at a time.

units.name: Thrall
units.sp: The lowest rank of the Cavalcade.

units.name: Captured Thrall
units.sp: Gained through the Throne of Worms (not bought). Do not count toward the maximum number of models (raising the cap from 13 to 18) and cannot be dismissed. Not really clever: Cannot earn Experience or gain advances.

units.name: Great Bear
units.sp: Large Target. Maddened With Pain: As soon as it suffers a single wound it gains an additional Attack. Animal: Uses no weapons or armour and never gains Experience.

units.name: Wild Beast
units.sp: Charge: +1 Attack on the first turn of close combat when it charges. Animal: Uses no weapons or armour and never gains Experience.

units.name: Fighting Ape
units.sp: Cymbals: May be given a pair of Cymbals (+15 gc) — enemies within 6" suffer −1 BS and −1 Ld. Agile: Has Scale Sheer Surfaces, Acrobat and Dodge, and may make a diving charge from up to 10" high. Animal: Never gains Experience but may climb and enter buildings normally.

reavers.name: Lustrian Reavers
reavers.rules: Hardened Old World survivors in Lustria (Tuomas Pirinen). Rare Heroes: You may only ever buy one of each Hero type. Choose any Hero as the Leader. Promotions: When a Hero is lost you cannot rebuy him — instead keep his equipment and promote a Prospect into that role (as a Lad's Got Talent result, with access to two of the warband's Hero skill lists; the new Hero takes one Advance roll immediately and inherits the dead Hero's gear). War Beasts (Beastmaster only) count toward the maximum warband size and must be bought at creation, but can be replaced if killed. Hired Swords: Only Ogre Bodyguard, Dwarf Trollslayer, Tilean Marksman or Big Game Hunter. Heroes start with special equipment (noted below) that cannot be replaced if lost. Humans (War Beasts are animals and do not advance).

units.name: Conqueror
units.sp: Starts with Masterwork Heavy Armour, Bec de Corbin and a Helmet. Survivor: Cannot be taken Out of Action unless already Knocked Down or Stunned (if standing when it would go Out of Action, treat as Stunned instead). The first time he rolls "Dead" on the Serious Injury chart, treat it as Multiple Injuries instead (once only).

units.name: Saurus Slayer
units.sp: Starts with Heavy Armour, Misericordia, two Swords and a Helmet. Duellist: When fighting a single opponent in melee he may re-roll missed hits on the first turn, whether he charged or was charged. May buy: Lizardmen Skin Cloak (10), Skull Mask (15), a Lizardman Sword upgrade (30) and a Trophy Slann Holy Headdress (35).

units.name: Reaver Beastmaster
units.sp: Starts with Heavy Armour, Spear and Sword. War Beasts: You may include up to 2 War Beasts (below); within 6" they may use the Beastmaster's Leadership.

units.name: Jungle Shadow
units.sp: Starts with Javelins, Light Armour and two Daggers. For +30 gc may become a Wizard (uses Lesser Magic, one random spell; if so, remove the starting Light Armour) — toggle "caster" below. Silent Hunter: Always counts as Hidden while touching terrain ≥1" high; shooting does not reveal him (spellcasting does). Surprise attack: Re-roll missed hits against enemies attacked from behind (180° rear arc). Canopy Walker: Automatically passes all climbing, jumping and falling tests.

units.name: Trapmaster
units.sp: Starts with Heavy Armour, Sword, a Tilean Hunting Rifle (treat as a Hochland Long Rifle), Firepots and a Leaf Coat (counts as an Elven Cloak). Deadeye: No penalty for shooting at long range. Traps: Starts with 1 Trap and may buy up to 5 more at 5 gc each (one use; place real+false counters within 3", a model within 2" flips one — a real trap deals D3 S5 hits, no criticals).

units.name: Estalian Warhound
units.sp: War Beast (Beastmaster only); counts toward the warband maximum. 5+ armour save from padded armour and iron collar. Animal: No weapons/armour and never gains Experience; injured exactly as a Henchman (dead on 1-2).

units.name: Barbary Monkey
units.sp: War Beast (Beastmaster only); counts toward the warband maximum. Nimble: Climbs any sheer surface without Initiative tests, re-rolls failed Diving Charge tests, and may enter buildings. Has a 5+ Ward save against any wound (melee, shooting, magic, falling). Animal: Never gains Experience; injured as a Henchman (dead on 1-2).

units.name: Tilean Hunting Hawk
units.sp: War Beast (Beastmaster only); counts toward the warband maximum. Part of the equipment: Acts with the Beastmaster and may attack any enemy within 12" (even out of line of sight, unless Hidden) then returns; it cannot be targeted separately, and does not attack while the Beastmaster is in close combat. Animal: Never gains Experience; injured as a Henchman (dead on 1-2).

units.name: Prospect
units.sp: Promotion Only: Re-roll any "The Lad's Got Talent" result. A Prospect can only become a Hero through the Promotions rule (replacing a lost Hero); when promoted you may hire a new Prospect.

maneaters.name: Maneaters
maneaters.rules: Ogre mercenaries (Border Town Burning). Fear: All Ogres cause fear except Youngbloods. Large: All Ogres are Large Targets (+1 to be hit, may be shot at even if not closest) except Youngbloods and Half-growns. Gluttony: Each Ogre counts as two models when selling wyrdstone/treasure; a captured model (or any own member/animal/mount) may be devoured — reduce the warband's model count by 1 (or 2 for a Large captive), and an Ogre Hero gains XP equal to models eaten. Slow Witted: Ogres need twice the usual XP to advance. Difficult Customers: Ogre Heroes get −1 to find Rare items that are not Ogre-exclusive. Cannibals: may hire no Hired Swords except Halflings and the Ogre Bodyguard. Ogres never use daggers. Uses the Ogre racial maximum.

units.name: Captain
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership. Large Target and causes Fear. Ogres carry no free dagger and never fight with daggers.

units.name: Youngblood
units.sp: Young Ogre: Does NOT cause Fear and is NOT a Large Target. No free dagger.

units.name: Mountain Guide
units.sp: Large Target and causes Fear. Ranger: If not put out of action, roll two dice for Exploration and pick one (not a re-roll). Loner: Immune to all All Alone tests, never claims a Gnoblar, and may never be the warband Leader. No free dagger.

units.name: Bull
units.sp: Large Target and causes Fear. Bull Charge: When charging may make a single attack at +1 to hit instead of its normal attacks; if it hits, the target is automatically knocked down. No free dagger.

units.name: Half-grown
units.sp: Causes Fear but is NOT a Large Target. No free dagger.

units.name: Sabretusk
units.sp: Requires a Mountain Guide (caged if none is in the warband). Trained: May use the Guide's Leadership within 6". Feral Instinct: At the start of your turn it must pass an Ld test or the opponent moves it this turn (it may even charge friendly models). Ignored: Out-of-action Sabretusks are not counted for Rout tests. Causes Fear. Animal: Never gains Experience.

caravans.name: Merchant Caravans
caravans.rules: Cathay-bound trade caravan (Border Town Burning). Merchant: The Merchant is always the warband Leader. Trade: instead of searching for rare items, the Merchant may sell a rare item stored in the Trade Wagon (roll D6: 1-2 half price, 3-4 full, 5-6 full + half). Open for Business: Any player may send a Hero to the Merchant to buy/sell one item if a price is agreed. Rarity: Any item reduced to Rare 2 or lower (Reputation, Streetwise, …) may be bought as Common. Hired Swords: may hire any available to Mercenaries.

units.name: Merchant
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership. He is always the warband leader; if he is lost, a new leader becomes a Merchant for all purposes (or buy an Apprentice to take over).

units.name: Apprentice
units.sp: May take over as Merchant/Leader if the Merchant is lost and no other model can.

units.name: Knights Vanguard
units.sp: Lightning Reflexes: When charged, strikes first (compare Initiative against chargers who also strike first). Ride Warhorse: Trained to ride a Warhorse. Hireling: Paid by the Merchant — may never become the warband Leader.

units.name: Magician
units.sp: Wizard: Uses the Lesser Magic spell list. Hireling: Paid by the Merchant — may never become the warband Leader.

units.name: Sell-sword
units.sp: Caravan bodyguard — roguish escort hired to protect the goods and their paymaster.

units.name: Marksman
units.sp: Tilean crossbow specialist.

units.name: Blackguard
units.sp: Strongman: May use a double-handed weapon without the "strike last" penalty. Unreliable Hirelings: May never become Heroes — re-roll any "The Lad's Got Talent" result.

units.name: Trade Wagon

attached.label: Draft Horse ×2
attached.note: Two draft horses pull the wagon (included in the price).

units.sp:
Wagon (Empire in Flames): Cannot charge, climb, hide, run or flee; may turn up to 45° at the end of its move. One model must drive (and may do nothing else while moving); one may ride shotgun and take over if the driver falls.
Movement (by load): 1 model (driver) M10 · 2-3 M8 · 4-6 M6. Apply the lash for +D6" (on a 1, roll Out of Control).
Shooting from it: Crew fire with a 360° arc at −1 to hit (moving surface). Shooting at it: +1 to hit (large target); on a hit roll Location — 1-2 a draft horse, 3 a crew member, 4-5 chassis (T8, 4W), 6 wheel (T6, 1W).
Collisions: Wagon vs wagon = both take D3 S7 hits; models in the path roll under their Initiative or take a S4 hit (out-of-action models are hit automatically). On any collision a D6 roll of 1 forces an Out of Control test.
Combat (stationary): Charge and strike the part in contact. Chassis and wheels are hit automatically. Crew: Count as defending an obstacle — a 6 is required to hit them regardless of Weapon Skill; if a crew member is taken Out of Action, an attacker may board the wagon next turn. Draft horses may be attacked normally or cut free on a 2+.
Combat (moving): may be charged (this does not halt it); roll Location and the crew still need a 6 to hit — attack from the side or rear (the front risks being run down). Fighters injured while aboard must pass an Initiative test or fall off.
Boarding: Board a stationary wagon by moving into contact; boarding a moving wagon needs an Initiative test (fail = D3 hits at Strength equal to half the wagon's speed).
Storage: Holds the warband's stored equipment and treasures (not gold) — lost if the wagon is destroyed. Reputation: +1 to find Rare items per five different rare items stored. Abandoned: If you fail a Rout test with no driver, the wagon and its contents fall to the enemy.

nightgoblins.name: Night Goblins
nightgoblins.rules: Cave-dwelling Night Goblins (Terry Maltman). Animosity: At the start of each turn roll a D6 — on a 1 the warband squabbles and does nothing else that turn. Only Night Goblins are affected (not Trolls, Squigs, Snotlings or Fanatics). Hate Stunties: Night Goblins hate Dwarfs (Fanatics are too far gone to be affected). Goblins use the Goblin racial maximum.

units.name: Big Boss
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership. Subject to Animosity and hates Dwarfs.

units.name: Night Goblin Shaman
units.sp: Wizard: Uses the Waaagh! Magic spell list. Subject to Animosity and hates Dwarfs.

units.name: Boss
units.sp: Subject to Animosity and hates Dwarfs.

units.name: Night Goblin
units.sp: Subject to Animosity and hates Dwarfs.

units.name: Fanatic
units.sp: Addict: Must buy Mad Cap Mushrooms before each game or he stays home foaming. Mushroom Brain: Gains no Experience. Looney: Subject to Frenzy and must test for permanent damage after the battle. Frantic: Always strikes first, ignoring weapon and Initiative modifiers. Not affected by Animosity or Hate Stunties.

units.name: Cave Squig
units.sp: Animal (gains no Experience). Movement: Roll 2D6" to move; never runs or charges, but contacting an enemy counts as a charge. Minderz: Must stay within 6" of a Goblin or it goes wild (moves 2D6" randomly, out of control). You may never field more Cave Squigs than Night Goblins.

units.name: Troll
units.sp: Fear. Stupidity. Large Target. Regeneration (4+; not vs fire). Dumb Monster: Gains no Experience and never rolls for injury after a battle. Always Hungry: 15 gc upkeep after each game (or sacrifice two Goblins/Squigs) or it wanders off. Vomit Attack: Instead of its normal attacks, one automatic Strength 5 hit that ignores armour saves.

units.name: Snotling Mob
units.sp: Bought as a mob of 5 (10 gc each, max 5). Pointy stick: Counts as a dagger (enemy +1 armour save). Mob: Must stay within 1" and fight together. Weedy: When wounded, knocked down on 1, stunned on 2-3, out of action on 4-6. Dodgy: 6+ dodge save vs shooting. Insignificant: The whole mob counts as a single model for Rout tests, warband size and income.

restless.name: The Restless Dead
restless.rules:
Liche-led undead (Border Town Burning).
Undead: All undead Cause Fear, are Immune to Psychology and Poison, treat stunned as knocked down (No Pain), and — except the Liche, Necromancer and Scarecrow — may not run.
Bone Goliath: The warband has access to this unique Hired Sword, bought during warband creation (not in this builder).
Racial maxima: Liche & Grave Guard/Wight use their own maxima; the Necromancer uses the Human maximum.

units.name: Liche
units.sp: Leader: Friendly models within 6" may use his Leadership. Wizard: Uses Necromancy (Restless Dead) and starts with two random spells. Carries no non-magical weapons (no penalty) but may wear armour. Cause Fear. Immune to Psychology (never leaves combat) and Poison. No Pain (stunned → knocked down). Eternal: May ignore any Serious Injury except "Killed" by taking a permanent −1 starting Wound (a "Killed" result costs −D3 starting Wounds; 0 or less = Killed). Feed Upon Magic: Between battles, consume D3 Treasures for a permanent +1 starting Wound (not if he searched rare items or went out of action last battle). Warrior Wizard: May wear armour and cast. Advancement: A +1 Wound advance may be swapped for a new skill.

units.name: Necromancer
units.sp: Wizard: Uses Necromancy (Restless Dead), starting with one of the spells the Liche knows. Apprentices: May only know spells the Liche knows (until the Liche dies, then learns freely). Gofer: When searching for rare items, roll 3D6 and pick the two highest.

units.name: Grave Guard
units.sp: Wight Blades: Any close-combat "to hit" roll of 6 wounds automatically (roll to wound only to check for a critical). Cause Fear. Immune to Psychology and Poison. No Pain. May not Run (but may charge). No deal: May not search for rare items.

units.name: Zombie
units.sp: No weapons or armour. Cause Fear. May not run (but may charge). Immune to Psychology and Poison. No Pain. No Brain: Gains no Experience.

units.name: Skeleton
units.sp: Cause Fear. May not run (but may charge). Immune to Psychology and Poison. No Pain. No Brain: Gains no Experience.

units.name: Wight
units.sp: Cause Fear. May not run (but may charge). Immune to Psychology and Poison. No Pain. May gain Experience; if promoted to a Hero it gains Wight Blades, must take the Combat and Strength skill lists, and (like Grave Guards) may not search for rare items.

units.name: Scarecrow
units.sp: Cause Fear. Immune to Psychology and Poison. No Pain. No Brain (no Experience). No Substance: Counts as Toughness 6 against shooting and magic missiles and is immune to shooting criticals (fire attacks deal damage normally). Flammable: Takes double damage from fire and catches fire on a 3+. Construct: May re-roll any Injury result except wounds caused by fire. Animated Construct: Controlled by the Liche or Necromancer (note which); each may control only one, so two Scarecrows need both. If its controller loses a wound, he must pass an unmodified Ld test or the Scarecrow is immediately taken out of action.
